rulings.Case Title: JOHN ORF et al VS. K & S CO., INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 14, 2010, line 15, PLAINTIFFS JOHN ORF, and, KYLE ORF Motion for Leave to File First Amended Complaint. Motion denied. Plaintiff has been dilatory and the proposed amendment pleads a new theory of liability against K&S on the eve of trial. Plaintiff was aware of the facts supporting the amendment no later than November 2009. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al VS. EILEEN BURKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default [Ccp 473(B)[
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 14, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT EILEEN BURKE (Amended) Notice of Motion for Relief from Default [CCP 473(B). Motion for relief from default is granted. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al VS. EILEEN BURKE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Prelimianry Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 14, 2010, line 9, Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction. Preliminary injunction granted. Plaintiff is likely to prevail on the merits. Chau listed the Folsom Street property at $1.39 million and has admitted the value. There was no trade of reasonably equivalent value. Plaintiff establishes liability because of an actual intent to hinder based on badges of fraud. (CC sec. 3439.04(b).) The balence of equities and harms are in plaintiff's favor. Chau has been thwarting the collection process for years and has been held in contempt. Burke accepted the property with knowledge of the pending suits. Court adopts the language of the proposed preliminary injunction, except page 3, line 18, modify "shall" to "may" be liable. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WAIKEAN GOH et al VS. SANDRA YANG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 14, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT SANDRA YANG DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 1st cause of action, unfair business practices, for plaintiffs to plead that they suffered lost money or property as a result of the unfair competition. Demurrer overruled as to all other causes of action. Settlement agreement not a matter that may be judicially noticed where the parties dispute the interpretation of the agreement. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WAIKEAN GOH et al VS. SANDRA YANG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 14, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT SANDRA YANG MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted with 10 days leave to amend as to claim for punitive damages only, for plaintiffs to plead malice, fraud, or oppression. Motion denied as moot as to the 2nd, 4th and 5th causes of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GALASSI ENTERPRISES,LLC VS. STEPHANIE O'DRISCOLL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 14, 2010, line 16, PLAINTIFF GALASSI ENTERPRISES, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion denied. No 60-day notice of habitual late payment is attached to the complaint. Heckman declaration is unsigned and inadmissible. Heckman email is inadmissible hearsay. Triable issue of fact whether alarm clock and smoke claims in violation of rental agreement. No admissible evidence that defendant used drugs or broke a window. Triable issue of fact regarding who broke the window and who stored items in the garage. Triable issue of fact whether premises occupied by anyone other than defendant. Even History detail reports are inadmissible hearsay. Triable issue of fact whether defendant caused noises after midnight in violation of lease agreement. Triable issue of fact whether defendant attempted to pay October rent and whether it was accepted by plaintiff. Triable issue of fact whether defendant freqently gives checks which are returned for insufficient funds. =(302/CWW)
